codeReview.summary: The round-2 state fixes the original missing callback forwarding and now persists mid-stream partials/tool-starts plus dedups bridge status broadcasts, but VAL-SPAWN-016 is still not fully satisfied. After a user selects a running bridge scoop, the extension client loads the pre-click buffer from SessionStore but does not restore the live relay's in-flight message id, so post-click deltas/tool results for that same turn can split into a second assistant message or be dropped entirely.

issues.description: `selectScoop()` now hydrates pre-click bridge-scoop state from `session-${scoop.folder}` (`packages/webapp/src/ui/main.ts:384-394`, `packages/webapp/src/ui/chat-panel.ts:159-177`), but `OffscreenClient.handleAgentEvent()` still discards all pre-selection agent events (`packages/webapp/src/ui/offscreen-client.ts:314`) and never seeds `currentMessageId` from the persisted assistant message it just loaded. The next post-click `text_delta` or `tool_start` therefore creates a brand-new message id (`offscreen-client.ts:318-332`), while `tool_end` / `response_done` are ignored if no id exists (`offscreen-client.ts:345-363`). Because `ChatPanel.handleMessageStart()` always appends a fresh assistant message and tool results only attach by message id (`packages/webapp/src/ui/chat-panel.ts:687-746`), a bridge scoop that emitted partial text or `tool_start` before selection can still lose its post-click continuation or tool result instead of continuing the already-hydrated turn. Example: pre-click `tool_start` persisted by `packages/chrome-extension/src/offscreen-bridge.ts:178-190`, then post-click `tool_end` at `offscreen-bridge.ts:201-220`, never renders a result because the selected panel has no matching live `currentMessageId`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture note now treats per-delta SessionStore persistence as the missing invariant for VAL-SPAWN-016, but the current failure boundary is larger: selection-time hydration also has to restore the live relay's in-flight message identity so later `text_delta` / `tool_end` / `response_done` events can continue the same turn.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:141-145 documents per-event persistence as the fix, while `packages/webapp/src/ui/offscreen-client.ts:313-363` and `packages/webapp/src/ui/chat-panel.ts:159-177,687-746` show there is still no handoff from the persisted message loaded by `switchToContext()` into the live `currentMessageId` relay state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The regression coverage for this bug class is still split across isolated layers and misses the real selection handoff: 'pre-click state persisted' plus 'post-click live events continue the same turn'. That gap let the current offscreen-client stitching bug survive both scrutiny round 1 and the follow-up fix.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `packages/chrome-extension/tests/offscreen-bridge.test.ts:341-421` only checks that `saveMessages` is called for pre-click partials/tool-starts, and `packages/webapp/tests/ui/offscreen-client.test.ts:93-117,357-379` only checks already-selected live event handling. There is no test that loads `session-agent-*` first and then drives post-selection `text_delta` / `tool_end` / `response_done` against that hydrated turn.

summary: Fail. `c6bf365` fixes pre-click persistence and duplicate status broadcasts, but the selected panel still cannot stitch post-click live events onto the pre-click turn loaded from SessionStore, so VAL-SPAWN-016 remains unsatisfied.
